text:
There was once an old man who loved collecting stories. He spent every free minute at the town library, pouring over every book he could grab. He amassed quite a library of anecdotes. When he finally retired, he decided to write a book of them, the title ominously suggesting "Fables of the Forgotten."

His editor suggested that the man start with a short story. The old man replied, "I have countless tales. Let me pick one." He thought for a moment, then said, "There was once a man who lived deep in a forgotten forest."

The story went, "Every morning he'd don his old-timey Sunday suit, grab his walking stick, and set out to explore. He had dreams—a big house nestled in a clearing, a garden brimming with blooms, and a library full of books."

One foggy morning, while wandering away from the familiar paths, he stumbled upon a clearing with flames flickering in an iron pot. The man approached and found it
